title: regreSSHion
subtitle: OpenSSH unauthenticated RCE
query: Triage CVE-2024-6387 (regreSSHion) in OpenSSH and tell me the remediation urgency.

data.query: Triage CVE-2024-6387 (regreSSHion) in OpenSSH and tell me the remediation urgency.

data.summary: CVE-2024-6387 ("regreSSHion") is a signal-handler race condition in OpenSSH sshd allowing unauthenticated remote code execution as root. It is a regression of CVE-2006-5051, reintroduced in OpenSSH 8.5p1 and fixed in 9.8p1.
data.recommended_action: SSVC: Attend — Public exploits exist (Exploit-DB #52269 + GitHub PoCs) and EPSS is 99.5% (99.94th percentile). Upgrade OpenSSH to 9.8p1+; macOS: 12.7.6 / 13.6.8 / 14.6. Interim: set LoginGraceTime 0 in sshd_config to break the race trigger. Restrict SSH to trusted IPs via firewall. Monitor for repeated failed auth attempts.

cves.summary: Race condition (signal-handler) in OpenSSH sshd allows an unauthenticated remote attacker to execute arbitrary code as root. Affects OpenSSH 8.5p1–9.7p1. A regression of CVE-2006-5051, fixed in OpenSSH 9.8p1.

reasoning_chain: cve_lookup(CVE-2024-6387) -> CVSS 8.1 HIGH, CWE-362 + CWE-364, signal-handler race in sshd enabling unauthenticated RCE as root
exploit_check(CVE-2024-6387) -> has_public_exploit=true; Exploit-DB #52269, 4 GitHub PoC repos
epss_score(CVE-2024-6387) -> probability=0.99506 (99.5%), percentile=0.9994 — near-certain exploitation likelihood
patch_lookup(CVE-2024-6387) -> fixed in OpenSSH 9.8p1; Apple macOS 12.7.6, 13.6.8, 14.6
attack_mapping([CWE-362, CWE-364]) -> no curated ATT&CK technique mappings returned
SSVC: Not in KEV but EPSS 99.5% + public exploits -> Attend (remediate faster than standard timelines)

ssvc.rationale: SSVC decision Act: CVE-2024-6387 a public exploit is available and EPSS predicts high near-term exploitation.
